data.content: This is an MSTest project on .NET 8. I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch` to run tests with hot reload. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. I can start `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start it up for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-fde17489c1474045892e559f12d5f4d1 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "sync", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: The watcher is running but there's a compile error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so the watch loop can actually run your tests.
data.reasoningText:
There's a build error — `ArgumentException` needs `System` to be imported. But the user said they have failing tests and want hot reload to iterate. The watch is running and waiting for changes. Let me fix the missing `using System;` so the tests can at least compile, then the watch will pick it up.

Actually, wait — the user said "I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?" They want me to set up the hot reload, not fix the tests. The watch is running but it's failing to compile. I need to fix the compile error so the watch can actually run.

Let me fix the missing `using System;` directive and the watch should pick up the change automatically.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping("express", ...)` returns `10.00`

The watcher is active — every time you save a file, tests will automatically re-run. To fix the failing test, you'd update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` from `10.00m` to `15.00m` (line 24). Want me to fix it?
